_meta.purpose: Measured hardware truth for this machine. All future design math cites THIS file, never spec sheets. Every number came from a hand-written C/CUDA probe in probes/ (instruments, exempt from the no-library rule). Best-of-N timing.
_meta.host: native Windows 10 -> WSL2 Ubuntu 26.04, gcc 15.2.0, CUDA 12.4 (nvcc), driver reports CUDA 13.0

cpu.probe: probes/cpu_flops.c (AVX2 256-bit FMA, 12 accumulators, OpenMP) + probes/ram_triad.c (STREAM triad)
cpu.note_flops: Sustained measured throughput, not a vendor peak. fp32 is a clean 2x fp64, confirming true 256-bit vectorization (~73% of Zen4 AVX2 peak under all-core thermal/freq limits). AVX-512 double-pumping could roughly 2x this; recorded as deferred, not claimed.

host_ram.kernel: STREAM triad a[i]=b[i]+s*c[i], 3 doubles moved/elem, 1.15 GiB working set (> LLC)
host_ram.note: Dual-channel DDR5 sustained; STREAM triad is typically 60-70% of theoretical channel peak.

gpu_vram_bandwidth.note: ~76% of the 504 GB/s spec peak; this measured number, not the spec, is the ceiling for the memory-bound thesis.

pcie.note: PCIe 4.0 x16. Pinned host memory ~1.6-2.1x faster than pageable. ~26.7 GB/s pinned is the effective host<->device link budget; the ~25 GB/s assumption in the brief is confirmed by measurement.

gpu_fp32_matmul.note: Hand-written kernels only. Tiled (16x16 shared memory) beats naive by 1.30x. Both are far below the card's fp32 arithmetic peak (~29 TFLOPs) because these are simple kernels with no register blocking / vectorized loads; that headroom is a known future rung, not a defect. Establishes our hand-written baseline.

gpu_bf16_tensorcore.note: DEFERRED, not faked. RTX 4070 (CC 8.9) has 4th-gen tensor cores with bf16/fp16 support, but a correct hand-written WMMA/mma probe is a later rung. Recorded null per standing order: no measurement, no number.
